summary: End-to-end backend + frontend testing of HRMIS MVP. All 26 backend pytest cases pass (auth, dashboards, employees, departments, attendance, leave, WFH, meetings, chat, announcements, notifications). Frontend smoke tested for both admin and employee experiences: login (with demo chip auto-fill), role-based routing, admin nav (10 items), Employees directory + Add dialog, Attendance monitor, Leave tabs, Announcements creation, Today check-in → check-out flow with live duration & status dropdown, Apply leave dialog, WFH apply, Chat (contacts + send message round-trip), Meetings schedule, Notification bell, role protection. Major flows are functional.

design_issues.screen: Admin Overview - Attendance · last 7 days chart
issues: Recharts emits 'width(-1) and height(-1)' console warning on initial mount - container sizing race. Visual renders fine after mount but produces noisy console warnings. Consider setting minHeight on ResponsiveContainer wrapper.
Demo account chips ('Super Admin', 'HR', 'Manager', 'Employee') lack data-testid attributes (e.g., demo-chip-admin). Works via text content but reduces test stability.
Heatmap visualization renders (30-day text present) but lacks a data-testid like 'attendance-heatmap' and the heatmap squares have no class/testid handle for automated testing.
Balance cards display values (e.g., Casual 10/12) but lack 'leave-balance-*' data-testid, making automation rely on text selectors.

action_items: Optional: Add data-testid attributes to demo-chip buttons on Login (e.g., demo-chip-super-admin, demo-chip-hr, demo-chip-manager, demo-chip-employee) for richer e2e testability.
Optional: Add data-testid='attendance-heatmap' on Profile heatmap container and per-cell testid for QA hooks.
Optional: Add data-testid='leave-balance-{type}' to balance cards on MyLeave.
Optional: Set minHeight on the recharts ResponsiveContainer on Admin Overview to silence the 'width(-1) height(-1)' warning on first paint.
critical_code_review_comments: Approve/Reject endpoints (/api/leave/{id}/approve, /api/leave/{id}/reject, /api/wfh/{id}/approve) require a non-optional JSON body (LeaveDecision/WFHDecision). Consider making the body Optional so a simple POST without payload also works (frontend usually sends just the action).
Leave types are case-sensitive ('Casual', 'Sick', 'Earned', 'WFH Quota'). Ensure frontend Apply Leave form uses the exact strings from /api/leave/balances; otherwise 'No balance for X' errors.
WFH apply uses a single 'date' field, not date range. Documentation/tests should reflect this; UI must submit one date per request.
Chat send payload uses 'body' not 'content' - confirm frontend uses 'body'.
Meetings payload field names are 'starts_at', 'ends_at', 'attendee_user_ids' - confirm frontend matches exactly.
test_credentials: Per /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Used admin@acme.com/Admin@123, maya@acme.com & diego@acme.com & lena@acme.com / Demo@123.
seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_-prefixed announcements, departments, leave/wfh requests, meetings, chat messages, employee accounts. Database is hrmis_database; cleanup not performed (data is namespaced with 'TEST_' prefix for easy identification).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the single regression entry point (run: pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v). Backend schemas reference: leave types are TitleCase ('Casual','Sick','Earned','WFH Quota'); approve/reject endpoints need JSON body {note: '...'}; WFH expects {date, reason}; meetings expect starts_at/ends_at/attendee_user_ids; chat send uses {to_user_id, body}; employee create needs 'designation' not 'title'. Frontend uses JWT in localStorage key 'hrmis_token'. Demo chips on login auto-fill via text but no data-testid. All admin & employee flows verified working including check-in → check-out cycle, status updates, leave/wfh applications, chat round-trip, role-based redirect from /admin to /employee.
